questions.question: Explain the principle of least privilege in Cloud Identity and Access Management (IAM). How does multi-factor authentication (MFA) enhance cloud security?
keyPoints: Least privilege grants minimum permissions required for task completion
MFA requires two or more verification factors
Reduces risk of unauthorized access via compromised credentials
IAM policies should be regularly audited and rotated

questions.question: Describe the differences between encryption at rest and encryption in transit in cloud environments. Why are both important for data protection?
keyPoints: Encryption at rest protects stored data on disks and databases
Encryption in transit secures data moving between users and cloud services
Both prevent data interception and unauthorized access
Cloud providers offer built-in encryption (AES-256, TLS 1.3)

questions.question: Discuss major cloud security compliance frameworks and regulations. How do organizations ensure compliance when using cloud services?
keyPoints: Shared responsibility model defines provider vs customer obligations
Regular audits, certifications, and compliance reports (SOC, ISO)
Data residency and sovereignty requirements must be addressed

questions.question: Explain Cloud Security Posture Management (CSPM) and Cloud Workload Protection Platform (CWPP). Discuss network security strategies for cloud environments including VPC, security groups, and Web Application Firewalls.
keyPoints: CSPM continuously monitors cloud configurations for misconfigurations and compliance
CWPP protects cloud workloads (containers, VMs, serverless) from threats
VPC provides network isolation; security groups act as virtual firewalls
WAF filters malicious HTTP traffic; DDoS protection and IDS/IPS enhance security
